senses.definition: an early form or first version of the Gospel; a hypothetical source from which later gospel narratives were derived
examples: Scholars discuss the protogospel as a theoretical precursor to the canonical Gospels.
The idea of a protogospel suggests there were earlier gospel-like messages circulating before the New Testament texts.

senses.definition: a preliminary or original draft of a text from which later copies are produced
examples: The protograph served as the basis for the final published edition.
Scholars compare the protograph with later copies to study text transmission.

senses.definition: the state in which a flower or organism develops female organs before male ones, affecting reproduction
examples: Protogynies can influence pollination patterns in certain plant species.
In some hermaphroditic animals, protogynies reduce the likelihood of self-fertilization.

senses.definition: describing an organism whose female reproductive parts mature before the male parts; relating to protogyny
examples: Protogynous species often show sequential hermaphroditism.
Some reef fish are protogynous, changing sex from female to male.

senses.definition: the condition of an organism beginning life as female and later becoming male; in plants, the maturation of female parts before male parts
examples: Protogyny is observed in several coral reef fish species.
In botany, protogyny refers to flowers whose female parts mature before the stamens.

senses.definition: an early or primitive hematoblast cell precursor in embryology
examples: Protohematoblasts are studied in early developmental biology to understand blood cell formation.
The term appears mainly in specialized embryology literature.

senses.definition: an early form or fossil stage related to true bugs (Hemiptera) in the evolutionary lineage
examples: Protohemiptera refer to ancient relatives of modern true bugs.
Fossils labeled protohemiptera help trace insect evolution.

senses.definition: relating to or denoting an early form of insects in the order Hemiptera
examples: Protohemipteran fossils illuminate early insect wing structure.
Researchers classified the specimen as protohemipteran.

senses.definition: an early form or developmental stage of Hemiptera-like organisms
examples: The specimen is sometimes described as protohemipteron in early insect classification.
Protohemipteron features are faintly visible in the fossil record.

senses.definition: relating to an early or primitive form of Hemiptera-like organisms
examples: Protohemipterous traits are observed in ancient fossils.
Scientists describe the lineage as protohemipterous.

senses.definition: an early or first leader or founder of a heresy (historical/theological context)
examples: Protoheresiarch is used in historical or theological discussions about early religious movements.
Scholars occasionally employ protoheresiarch to refer to an initial leader of a heretical group.

senses.definition: an early or primitive horse-like mammal from the fossil record
examples: Protohippus is an ancestor of modern horses in paleontological classifications.
Fossils of protohippus help trace horse evolution.

senses.definition: a researcher who studies the period before written history or the earliest historical records
examples: Protohistorian studies attempt to reconstruct societies before writing.
The term is used more in academic discussions than in everyday speech.

senses.definition: plural of protohistorian; researchers who study prehistory
examples: Protohistorians debate how early humans organized societies.
The conference included several protohistorians presenting new finds.

senses.definition: relating to a period before recorded history or to events that occurred before written records
examples: Protohistoric times refer to the era before written records were kept.
Archaeologists study protohistoric sites to learn about early human life.

senses.definition: early or preliminary historical narratives or records from prehistory
examples: Protohistories provide fragments of how ancient peoples described their world.
Scholars compare protohistories across regions to understand migration patterns.

senses.definition: the period or study of history before written records; the prehistory
examples: Protohistory covers the times before written documents.
Researchers examine artefacts to learn about protohistory.

senses.definition: an early, transitional form in the genus Homo in human evolution
examples: Protohomo refers to early relatives in the human lineage.
Paleontologists classify certain fossils as protohomo.

senses.definition: an early or primitive human being or creature in evolutionary terms
examples: Protohuman remains help scientists understand early human development.
The term protohuman is used in discussions of pre-human ancestors.

senses.definition: early humans or human ancestors in evolutionary studies
examples: Protohumans show gradual changes leading to modern Homo sapiens.
Researchers debate the behaviors of protohumans.

senses.definition: an early form of a hydra-like organism in evolutionary biology
examples: Protohydra would be an ancient relative of modern cnidarians.
Fossils labeled protohydra provide clues about early multicellular life.

senses.definition: an early or hypothetical form of hydrogen in theoretical chemistry or cosmology
examples: Protohydrogen is a theoretical concept in models of the early universe.
Some estimates discuss protohydrogen in primordial star formation theories.

senses.definition: an early or primitive member of the order Hymenoptera (bees, ants, wasps) in evolutionary terms
examples: Protohymenoptera refers to early hymenopteran insects in the fossil record.
Paleontologists study protohymenoptera to understand the evolution of social insects.

senses.definition: relating to an early or primitive member of the order Hymenoptera
examples: Protohymenopteran features are seen in ancient fossils.
Researchers describe protohymenopteran specimens with simple wing structures.

senses.definition: an early form or species within the suborder Hymenoptera in the fossil record
examples: A protohymenopteron specimen was described in the new fossil study.
Protohymenopteron helps trace the diversification of wasps and allies.

senses.definition: pertaining to an early or primitive Hymenoptera-like organism
examples: Protohymenopterous traits appear in early insect fossils.
The study discusses protohymenopterous lineage features.

senses.definition: a very early leader or originator of a heresy; a founder of an early heretical movement
examples: Scholars used protoheresiarch to describe a figure who started a heretical sect.
The term is mainly used in historical or theological analyses.

senses.definition: an early, primitive horse-like animal in the evolution of horses
examples: Protohippus is an extinct ancestor of modern horses.
Fossils of protohippus help map the horse family tree.

senses.definition: a scholar who studies times before written history
examples: A protohistorian examines artifacts to learn about prehistory.
Protohistorian research often involves archaeology and anthropology.

senses.definition: plural of protohistorian; scholars who study prehistory
examples: Protohistorians presented new artefact-based theories at the conference.
Different protohistorians may interpret the same site differently.

senses.definition: relating to or occurring before recorded history
examples: Protohistoric periods predate written records.
Archaeologists study protohistoric sites to learn about early societies.

senses.definition: early historical records or accounts from prehistory
examples: Protohistories include artefacts and oral traditions from before writing.
Scholars compare protohistories across regions.

senses.definition: the period before written history; the study of such times
examples: Protohistory covers the years before written records began.
Researchers piece together protohistory from artefacts and legends.

senses.definition: an early member of the genus Homo in human evolution
examples: Protohomo refers to an ancient form in the human lineage.
Fossils labeled protohomo shed light on early human development.

senses.definition: an early or primitive human being in evolutionary terms
examples: Protohuman remains help scientists understand early human evolution.
The term is used in paleoanthropology.

senses.definition: early humans or human ancestors in evolutionary studies
examples: Protohumans show how humans evolved over millions of years.
Researchers compare protohumans across continents.

senses.definition: an ancient, primitive hydrozoan-like organism related to the hydra
examples: Protohydra represents an early form of colonial organisms.
Fossils of protohydra help scientists study early aquatic life.

senses.definition: an early or hypothetical form of hydrogen in theoretical contexts
examples: Protohydrogen is discussed in some cosmology models.
The concept is used mainly in theoretical chemistry discussions.

senses.definition: an early form or evolutionary stage of the order Hymenoptera (bees, wasps, ants)
examples: Protohymenoptera fossils illuminate early insect evolution.
Researchers discuss protohymenoptera in early arthropod studies.

senses.definition: relating to or characteristic of an early or primitive Hymenoptera
examples: Protohymenopteran features are visible in the oldest fossils.
The study cites protohymenopteran traits in early insect lineages.

senses.definition: an early or primitive species within the Hymenoptera lineage
examples: The protohymenopteron specimen helps trace diversification of wasps.
This find is described as a protohymenopteron in the fossil record.

senses.definition: relating to an early or primitive Hymenoptera-like organism
examples: Protohymenopterous traits appear in ancient insect fossils.
The paper discusses protohymenopterous lineage features.

senses.definition: an early or primitive member of the Hymenoptera order
examples: The protohymenopteron fossil provides clues to early insect evolution.
Paleontologists classify this specimen as protohymenopteron.
